Official name: Modernizing Retrospective Regulatory Review
Casual name: Modernizing Retrospective Regulatory Review
Short summary: Requires agencies to use AI in retrospective reviews of regulations. Directs the Office of Management and Budget to issue guidance on AI use for identifying obsolete or burdensome regulations. Mandates agency plans and implementation strategies for these AI-assisted reviews.
Long summary:
Requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to report on the Federal Government's progress in making agency regulations available in machine-readable format within 180 days after enactment.

Mandates the OMB Director to issue guidance within 18 months on using technology, including AI, for efficient and accurate retrospective reviews of regulations.

Obliges agency heads to submit a detailed plan within two years for implementing OMB guidance on retrospective reviews, identifying regulations for review, and including necessary data and analysis.

Directs agency heads to implement the submitted plan within 180 days of submission, focusing on retrospective regulatory reviews.
Tags: Strategies: Government study or report;Strategies: Governance development;Strategies: Governance development;Strategies: Government study or report;Strategies: Governance development

Official name: Veterans Appeals Efficiency Act of 2024
Casual name: Veterans Appeals Efficiency Act of 2024
Short summary: Requires the Secretary to track and maintain information on certain veterans' benefits claims using technology. Permits the Board of Veterans' Appeals to aggregate appeals with similar legal or factual questions. Allows AI use in studying common legal questions.
Long summary:
Requires the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to submit an annual report on the average length of time remanded claims are pending.

Mandates the use of technology to track and maintain information on specific claims for benefits, including those continuously pursued, in the National Work Queue, or pending a hearing.

Ensures claimants can view reasons for claim deferrals or suspense dates in the National Work Queue.

Authorizes the Chairman of the Board of Veterans’ Appeals to aggregate appeals with similar questions of law or fact.

Obliges the Board to ensure substantial compliance with its remand decisions, allowing waivers if sufficient evidence resolves underlying issues.

Grants the Court of Appeals for Veterans Claims supplemental jurisdiction to review eligible claims and remand matters for addressing unaddressed issues or inadequate decisions by the Board.

Directs a study, potentially using AI, on common legal or factual questions before the Board and mandates a report on the findings.

Requires the Secretary to develop a plan for timely assignment of claims and submit it to Congress.

Official name: Preserving Telehealth, Hospital, and Ambulance Access Act, Sec. 105 (Report on Wearable Medical Devices)
Casual name: Preserving Telehealth, Hospital, and Ambulance Access Act, Sec. 105 (Report on Wearable Medical Devices)
Short summary: Requires the Comptroller General to assess and report on wearable medical devices' capabilities and limitations in clinical decision-making, focusing on AI's benefits and challenges, and proposing policy options to enhance benefits and mitigate challenges.
Long summary:
Mandates the Comptroller General to conduct a technology assessment of wearable medical devices within 18 months of the Act's enactment.

Requires the report to include an analysis of the devices' potential to accurately prescribe treatments.

Examines the benefits and challenges of using artificial intelligence to augment wearable medical devices.

Proposes policy options to enhance benefits and mitigate challenges in developing or using such devices.

Official name: AI CONSENT Act
Casual name: AI CONSENT Act
Short summary: Requires companies to obtain informed consent from consumers before using their data for training AI systems.
Long summary:
Requires companies to obtain informed consent from consumers before using their data to train AI systems.

Requires companies to explain clearly and concisely how consumer data will be used for AI training.

Mandates disclosure of further data usage, including transfers to third-party entities.

Directs the FTC to issue a report on the progress of converting covered data to de-identified data (for Congress' evaluation).

Treats violations as unfair or deceptive acts under the Federal Trade Commission Act and subjects violators to penalties.
Tags: Risk factors: Transparency;Strategies: Disclosure;Strategies: Disclosure: In deployment;Risk factors: Security;Risk factors: Privacy;Risk factors: Privacy;Risk factors: Security;Risk factors: Transparency;Strategies: Disclosure;Strategies: Disclosure: In deployment;Strategies: Disclosure: About inputs;Strategies: Governance development;Strategies: Government study or report;Strategies: Evaluation;Incentives: Fines;Incentives: Civil liability;Risk factors: Privacy;Risk factors: Security

Official name: ENFORCE Act
Casual name: ENFORCE Act
Short summary: Amends the Export Control Reform Act to prevent foreign adversaries from exploiting U.S. AI technologies. Defines "covered AI systems" posing national security risks. Grants the President authority to control activities related to these systems. Requires licenses for certain exports and related activities.
Long summary:
Amends the Export Control Reform Act of 2018 to prevent foreign adversaries from exploiting U.S. artificial intelligence and other critical technologies.

Defines "artificial intelligence" and "artificial intelligence system" as per the National Artificial Intelligence Initiative Act of 2020.

Specifies an interim and final definition of "covered artificial intelligence system," focusing on AI systems posing serious national security risks.

Authorizes the President to control the activities of U.S. persons related to critical AI systems and emerging technologies essential to national security.

Empowers the President to require U.S. persons to obtain licenses for exporting or transferring critical AI systems and technologies.

Amends the International Emergency Economic Powers Act to align with the updated export control provisions.
Tags: Harms: Harm to health/safety;Risk factors: Security;Risk factors: Security: Cybersecurity;Strategies: Input controls;Strategies: Licensing, registration, and certification;Strategies: Input controls

Official name: Examining Educational Redlining in Lending Act
Casual name: Examining Educational Redlining in Lending Act
Short summary: Requires the Bureau of Consumer Financial Protection to annually assess the use of educational data and automated processes in creditworthiness determinations. Mandates reporting to Congress and public disclosure of findings and involved entities to prevent disparate impacts on protected classes.
Long summary:
Requires the Bureau of Consumer Financial Protection to assess the use of educational data and automated processes in creditworthiness determinations annually.

Mandates coordination with executive agencies and civil rights stakeholders for these assessments.

Examines the risks and disparate impacts of using automated or algorithmic underwriting processes.

Obligates the Bureau to report findings and recommendations to Congress 60 days post-assessment.

Requires public disclosure of assessment findings, lists of covered persons using educational data, and those employing automated or algorithmic processes.

Defines key terms, including "certain educational data" and "covered person," with references to existing legislation.

Official name: American Economic Independence Act of 2024
Casual name: American Economic Independence Act
Short summary: Requires regular reports on U.S.-China economic integration, assessing national security risks, including reliance on Chinese AI. Mandates analysis of risks like intellectual property theft and defense industrial base threats. Recommends mitigation steps and involves key government officials in the evaluation process.
Long summary:
Requires the President, through the Director of the Office of Management and Budget, to submit a report to Congress on the state of economic integration between the U.S. and China, and the national security risks posed by this integration, every three years for 15 years.

Mandates each report to assess the level of economic integration and its changes since 2000, with predictions for the next three years, specifically in priority sectors including artificial intelligence.

Includes analysis of risks such as reliance on Chinese entities, supply chain dependencies, intellectual property theft, and impacts on the U.S. defense industrial base, particularly in the AI sector.

Requires recommendations for mitigating identified risks.

Permits consultation with nongovernmental entities and submission of the report in an unclassified form, potentially with a classified annex.

Official name: Contraband Awareness Technology Catches Harmful Fentanyl Act
Casual name: CATCH Fentanyl Act
Short summary: Establishes pilot projects for testing AI and other technology enhancements to improve contraband detection at U.S. land ports of entry. Requires assessments of technology effectiveness, integration, and cost. Involves private sector input and mandates reporting to Congress on outcomes and recommendations.
Long summary:
Mandates the Secretary of Homeland Security, through the CBP Innovation Team, to establish pilot projects within one year to test and assess technologies enhancing nonintrusive inspection systems at land ports of entry.

Requires testing of at least five technology types, including artificial intelligence, machine learning, and quantum information sciences, to detect contraband, illegal drugs, and threats.

Identifies effective enhancements based on detection accuracy, efficiency, integration capability, cost, safety, and mobility.

Allows private sector input on commercially viable technologies.

Sets termination of pilot projects five years post-enactment.

Requires reporting to Congress within three years and after project termination, detailing effectiveness, recommendations, cost plans, and performance analysis of tested technologies.

Official name: Promoting United States Leadership in Standards Act of 2024
Casual name: US Leadership in Standards Act
Short summary: Directs the National Institute of Standards and Technology to increase U.S. participation in AI standards development. Establishes a pilot program to support U.S. meetings on AI standards. Requires web portal creation and congressional briefings on progress and effectiveness.
Long summary:
Directs the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) to brief Congress on opportunities for U.S. participation in developing AI standards within one year.

Requires the establishment of a web portal to inform U.S. industry and federal agencies about international AI standards efforts and participation opportunities.

Mandates federal agencies to report their participation in AI standards activities using a mechanism developed by the NIST.

Initiates a pilot program to support U.S.-based AI standards meetings through grants to eligible entities, with funding covering up to 50% of meeting costs.

Authorizes $10 million for the pilot program from fiscal years 2024 through 2028.

Official name: AI PLAN Act
Casual name: AI PLAN Act
Short summary: Requires the Secretaries of the Treasury, Homeland Security, and Commerce to submit an annual report to Congress on the risks of use of AI in the commission of financial crimes.
Long summary:
Requires the Secretary of the Treasury, Secretary of Homeland Security, and Secretary of Commerce to submit a joint report to Congress on the risks of use of AI in financial crimes, including fraud and misinformation, within 180 days of enactment and annually thereafter.

Mandates the inclusion in the report of interagency policies, procedures, and resources needed to defend against AI-driven financial crimes.

Directs the report to include the consideration of risks such as deepfakes, voice cloning, foreign election interference, synthetic identities, false market signals, and overall digital fraud.

Requires the subsequent submission of legislative recommendations and best practices to address risks identified within 90 days of each report.
Tags: Harms: Detrimental content;Harms: Financial loss;Strategies: Government study or report;Applications: Finance and investment;Harms: Violation of civil or human rights, including privacy;Strategies: Governance development

Official name: National Patient Safety Board Act of 2024
Casual name: National Patient Safety Board Act of 2024
Short summary: Establishes the National Patient Safety Board (NPSB) to prevent patient safety events. Empowers NPSB to monitor, study, and propose solutions. Grants authority to request changes and review reports. Mandates annual audits. Forms Patient Safety Research Division. Requires privacy and AI compliance. Prohibits use in civil cases.
Long summary:
Establishes the National Patient Safety Board (NPSB) within the Office of the Secretary of Health and Human Services to prevent and reduce patient safety events.

Empowers the NPSB to support federal agencies in monitoring patient safety, studying causes of patient safety events, and formulating preventive solutions.

Grants the NPSB authority to request changes to patient safety measures, review reports, and make them publicly available within one year of study commencement.

Mandates annual audits and reports to Congress on progress, including detailed solutions and unaddressed needs.

Forms a Patient Safety Research and Development Division to aggregate and analyze patient-level data, identifying and anticipating safety events and disparities.

Requires compliance with privacy laws and AI governance frameworks, including NIST's AI Risk Management Framework and the Blueprint for an AI Bill of Rights.

Prohibits the use of NPSB reports as evidence in civil liability cases but allows their use in developing standards of care.

Official name: Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act
Casual name: Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act
Short summary: Directs the NIST to establish task forces to develop standards for identifying AI-generated content. Mandates disclosures of AI origins through robust watermarks and content provenance metadata.
Long summary:
Directs the NIST to establish task forces focused on the development of technical guidelines for identifying and labeling AI-generated content, including metadata, watermarking, and digital fingerprinting.

Supports the development of standards to provide cryptographically verifiable content provenance metadata and robust watermarks. 

Requires providers of generative AI applications to ensure that content created or modified by such applications includes a disclosure of its AI origin, and to collaborate with providers of covered online platforms to provide these disclosures to users.

Prohibits online platforms from removing such disclosures and mandates clear display of AI origin information to users.

Enforces compliance under the Federal Trade Commission Act.

Defines self-regulatory guidelines approved by the FTC as compliance alternatives.

Official name: Emerging Innovative Border Technologies Act
Casual name: Emerging Innovative Border Technologies Act
Short summary: Requires the Secretary of Homeland Security to develop a plan for identifying, integrating, and deploying advanced technologies, including AI, to enhance border security. Involves privacy impact assessments, CBP Innovation Teams, and collaboration with private and public sectors.
Long summary:
Requires the Secretary of Homeland Security to develop and submit a plan within 180 days to identify, integrate, and deploy advanced technologies, including AI and machine learning, to enhance border security operations.

Mandates inclusion of information on how CBP utilizes its Innovation Teams and other mechanisms to achieve these goals.

Requires assessments of technology contributions, team compositions, and coordination efforts within CBP and DHS.

Identifies non-utilized technologies from other federal agencies that could enhance border security.

Includes an analysis of procurement authorities and whether additional ones are needed.

Demands explanations on scaling existing programs and descriptions of planned security technology programs with objectives, goals, and timelines.

Assesses the privacy and security impact on border communities and legacy technology phase-outs.

Requires coordination with DHS's Science and Technology Directorate to develop and incentivize new technologies.

Specifies metrics for evaluating effectiveness and identifies recent technological advancements in various surveillance and inspection technologies.

Authorizes CBP Innovation Teams to research and adapt commercial technologies, establishing procedures and strategic goals.

Requires annual reporting on Innovation Team activities, including successful technology transitions.

Official name: Richard L. Trumka Protecting the Right to Organize Act of 2023, Sec. 309 (GAO Report)
Casual name: Richard L. Trumka Protecting the Right to Organize Act of 2023, Sec. 309 (GAO Report)
Short summary: Analyze the impact of Sections 101(a) and 101(b) on technology and algorithm use. Require the Government Accountability Office to report findings to Congress. Allow the President to recommend legislative modifications based on these findings.
Long summary:
Mandates the Government Accountability Office (GAO) to study the impact of Sections 101(a) and 101(b) on employee coverage under the National Labor Relations Act, focusing on union formation and collective bargaining.

Requires the study to analyze impacts on employee flexibility, compensation, grievance resolution, and technology use, including AI and algorithms.

Directs the GAO to gather input from stakeholders, including business enterprises and labor organizations, consulting relevant congressional committees.

Obliges the GAO to report its findings to the House and Senate committees six months after the study begins and make the report public.

Instructs the President, after the report's release, to consider its findings and potentially recommend modifications to Sections 101(a) or 101(b) within 60 days.

Expresses the House's intent to evaluate any presidential recommendations regarding the modifications.

Official name: Americas Act, Title II ("Trade and Investment for the Americas"), Sec. 255 ("Transformational Energy Development")
Casual name: Americas Act, Title II ("Trade and Investment"), Sec. 255 ("Energy Development")
Short summary: Establishes a Chief Energy Officer to promote transformational energy technologies, including AI, in energy infrastructure. Requires 30% of project support for these technologies. Mandates annual reporting on such projects. Creates a database for international energy finance coordination.
Long summary:
Amends the BUILD Act of 2018 to define "transformational energy technology," including AI applications for energy efficiency and grid operation.

Inserts a Chief Energy Officer position within the U.S. International Development Finance Corporation (DFC) to promote transformational energy technology exports and ensure at least 30% of DFC support targets such projects.

Requires annual and quarterly reports on transformational energy technology projects and international finance of energy generation.

Establishes an Office of Energy in the Export-Import Bank to promote and finance exports of eligible technologies, including those involving AI.

Mandates the creation of a Joint Energy Export, Development, and Trade Database to coordinate inter-agency efforts.

Increases the DFC's maximum contingent liability and adjusts the Export-Import Bank’s loan, guarantee, and insurance authority limits.

Official name: Coast Guard Authorization Act of 2024, Title III ("Shipping and Navigation"), Sec. 342 ("Establishment of National Advisory Committee on Autonomous Maritime Systems")
Casual name: Coast Guard Authorization Act, Title III ("Shipping and Navigation"), Sec. 342 ("Advisory Committee on Autonomous Maritime Systems")
Short summary: Establishes a National Advisory Committee on Autonomous Maritime Systems to advise on regulation and use within U.S. waters. Requires 9 expert members representing diverse maritime sectors, including marine safety, vessel design, and unmanned vehicle research, appointed by the Secretary.
Long summary:
Establishes the National Advisory Committee on Autonomous Maritime Systems.

Advises the Secretary on regulation and use of Autonomous Systems within U.S. territorial waters.

Comprises 9 members appointed by the Secretary with relevant expertise.

Ensures representation from various groups, including marine safety, vessel design, unmanned vehicle production, port authorities, vessel operators, labor unions, maritime pilots, commercial space operators, and academic institutions.

Mandates establishment of the Committee within 90 days post-enactment.

Official name: Countering Communist China Act, Title VII ("Matters Related to Defense"), Sec. 701 ("Modifications to Use of Emergency Sanctions Authorities Regarding Communist Chinese Military Companies")
Casual name: Countering Communist China Act, Title VII ("Defense"), Sec. 701 ("Emergency Sanctions Regarding Chinese Military Companies")
Short summary: Requires amendments to emergency sanctions on Chinese military companies to includes sectors like AI.
Long summary:
Mandates the exercise of emergency sanctions authorities regarding Communist Chinese military companies.

Defines affected companies as those engaged in commercial services, manufacturing, producing, or exporting in the AI sector.
Tags: Strategies: Input controls

Official name: Countering Communist China Act, Title I ("Matters Related to Trade, Investment, and Economic Relations"), Sec. 101 ("Preventing Adversaries from Developing Critical Capabilities")
Casual name: Countering Communist China Act, Title I ("Trade, Investment, and Economic Relations"), Sec. 101 ("Preventing Adversaries from Developing Critical Capabilities")
Short summary: Identify categories of technologies and prohibit certain activities in sectors related to AI development that that poses acute threats to US national security.
Long summary:
Requires the President to identify, list, and annually update categories of technologies and products in the AI sector that pose acute threats to U.S. national security if acquired by adversaries.

Requires the President to prescribe regulations prohibiting U.S. persons from engaging in covered activities involving listed technologies and products in the AI development sector.

Directs the President to submit annual congressional reports on notifications of covered activities and assessing the need for regulatory amendments.

Encourages multilateral engagement to align with allied countries on similar mechanisms for prohibitions and notifications on technologies in the AI sector.

Authorizes $25 million for implementation and allows direct hiring to administer the entire Act.

Official name: Coast Guard Authorization Act of 2024, Title II ("Coast Guard"), Sec. 207 ("Report on establishment of unmanned systems capabilities office")
Casual name: Coast Guard Authorization Act, Title II ("Coast Guard"), Sec. 207 ("unmanned systems capabilities office")
Short summary: Require the Coast Guard to report on establishing an office for unmanned systems technologies. Include strategies for management, service-wide coordination, contracting, collaboration, and data sharing. Develop operational concepts integrating AI and machine learning tools.
Long summary:
Mandates the Commandant to submit a report within one year outlining a plan for establishing an unmanned systems capabilities office within the Coast Guard.

Requires the report to include a management strategy for the acquisition, development, and deployment of unmanned and counter-unmanned system technologies.

Specifies the need for a service-wide coordination strategy to synchronize efforts across the Coast Guard.

Identifies contracting and acquisition authorities necessary to expedite technology development and deployment.

Lists commercially available technologies useful for the Coast Guard.

Calls for a cross-agency collaboration plan with the Department of Homeland Security, the Department of Defense, and other relevant agencies.

Highlights opportunities to share unmanned system data to improve maritime domain awareness.

Requires the development of a concept of operations for a data ecosystem supporting unmanned systems, including AI and machine learning tools.

Official name: Farm, Food, and National Security Act of 2024, Title VIII ("Forestry"), Sec. 8414 ("Public-Private Wildfire Technology Deployment and Testbed Partnership")
Casual name: Farm, Food, and National Security Act, Title VIII ("Forestry"), Sec. 8414 ("Wildfire Technology Deployment and Testbed Partnership")
Short summary: Establishes a pilot program for deploying and testing wildfire prevention technologies, prioritizing AI and emerging technologies. Requires collaboration between federal agencies and private entities. Mandates annual reports on technologies tested and potential large-scale adoption. Program expires September 30, 2029.
Long summary:
Establishes a deployment and testbed pilot program for new and innovative wildfire prevention, detection, communication, and mitigation technologies.

Requires incorporation of the Pilot Program into an existing interagency coordinating group on wildfires.

Mandates consultation with covered agencies to identify key technology priority areas.

Partners with selected covered entities for real-time and on-the-ground testing of technology during wildfire mitigation activities.

Prioritizes emerging technologies, including artificial intelligence, quantum sensing, and augmented reality, for wildfire mitigation.

Requires submission of annual reports to appropriate committees, detailing participating entities, tested technologies, costs, outreach efforts, and assessments.

Expires the Pilot Program on September 30, 2029.

Official name: Department of State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs Appropriations Act 2024, Title VII ("General Provisions"), Sec. 7032 ("Democracy Programs")
Casual name: DOS Foreign Operations Appropriations Act, Title VII ("General Provisions"), Sec. 7032 ("Democracy Programs")
Short summary: Include AI challenges in the President's democracy strategy within 180 days, focusing on digital security, countering disinformation, and AI-related democracy threats. Allocate funds for digital security and counter-disinformation initiatives as part of broader democracy programs.
Long summary:
Requires the allocation of $2,900,000,000 for democracy programs from various appropriations, including $159,240,000 for the Bureau of Democracy, Human Rights, and Labor.

Mandates the submission of a comprehensive five-year strategy for democracy promotion abroad within 180 days, addressing digital security, countering disinformation, and AI-related challenges.

Prohibits foreign governments from having prior approval over the implementation of democracy programs and protects the confidentiality of implementing partners in undemocratic countries.

Allocates $30,000,000 to protect civil society activists and journalists and $20,000,000 for international freedom of expression and independent media.

Requires the regular informing of the National Endowment for Democracy about planned and supported democracy programs.

Official name: Countering Communist China Act, Title VI ("Matters Related to Democracy, Human Rights, and Taiwan"), Sec. 615 ("Deterring America's Technological Adversaries Act")
Casual name: Countering Communist China Act, Title VI ("Democracy, Human Rights, and Taiwan"), Sec. 615 ("DATA Act")
Long summary:
Prohibits U.S. persons from engaging in transactions involving sensitive personal data with entities subject to Chinese influence.

Requires the President to impose sanctions on foreign persons dealing in connected software applications under Chinese control, facilitating military, espionage, or malicious cyber activities.

Mandates sanctions for foreign persons involved in election interference against the U.S. or its allies, or influencing U.S. policy in favor of China.

Directs the President to determine and report on whether entities like ByteDance and TikTok meet criteria for sanctions.

Empowers the President to block all transactions involving property of sanctioned foreign persons within U.S. jurisdiction.

Allows the President to waive sanctions on a case-by-case basis if vital to U.S. national security interests.

Terminates the section's authorities five years after enactment.

Official name: Farm, Food, and National Security Act of 2024, Title VI ("Rural Development"), Sec. 6303 ("Promoting Precision Agriculture")
Casual name: Farm, Food, and National Security Act, Title VI ("Rural Development"), Sec. 6303 ("Precision Agriculture")
Short summary: Requires the Secretary to develop voluntary interconnectivity standards for precision agriculture, considering AI impacts. Directs coordination with industry and government stakeholders. Mandates GAO to assess these standards biannually for 8 years, evaluating voluntary adoption and industry coordination.
Long summary:
Promotes United States leadership in developing standards for precision agriculture.

Mandates the Secretary to develop voluntary, consensus-based interconnectivity standards for precision agriculture within two years.

Requires consultation with NIST, FCC, public, and private stakeholders, including voluntary consensus standards development organizations.

Considers the impact of advanced wireless communications technology and artificial intelligence on precision agriculture.

Addresses cybersecurity challenges and connectivity needs of precision agriculture technology.

Directs the Comptroller General to assess the developed standards one year after implementation and biennially for eight years.

Obligates the Comptroller General to report findings to relevant Senate and House committees.

Official name: Countering Communist China Act, Title I ("Matters Related to Trade, Investment, and Economic Relations"), Sec. 102 ("Sanctions with Respect to Communist Chinese Military and Surveillance Companies")
Casual name: Countering Communist China Act, Title I ("Trade, Investment, and Economic Relations"), Sec. 102 ("Sanctions for Chinese Military and Surveillance Companies")
Short summary: Requires the President to impose sanctions on AI development activities and ownership by foreign persons to knowingly be involved/associated with the Chinese military and surveillance apparatus.
Long summary:
Requires the President to impose sanctions on foreign persons engaged in significant operations in China's defense or surveillance technology sectors.

Focuses particular attention on foreign persons engaged in AI, machine learning, robotics, quantum information science, and other advanced technologies.

Utilizes the International Emergency Economic Powers Act to block and prohibit transactions involving sanctioned foreign persons' property within U.S. jurisdiction.
Tags: Strategies: Input controls;Strategies: Input controls;Strategies: Input controls;Incentives: Criminal liability;Incentives: Civil liability

Official name: Farm, Food, and National Security Act of 2024, Title VII ("Research, Extension, and Related Matters"), Sec. 7208 ("Centers of Excellence")
Casual name: Farm, Food, and National Security Act, Title VII ("Research and Extension"), Sec. 7208 ("Centers of Excellence")
Short summary: Establishes centers of excellence for research on digital agriculture, including AI and remote sensing. Requires partnerships to enhance coordination and address agricultural challenges. Mandates annual reporting on projects, funding, and intellectual property actions. Extends program duration to 2029.
Long summary:
Mandates the Secretary of Agriculture to establish at least one center of excellence to conduct research, extension, and education activities.

Designates eligible host institutions, including land-grant colleges and accredited veterinary schools, and requires geographic diversity in selection.

Limits each institution to host or co-host only one center at a time.

Requires partnerships with federal agencies, state governments, higher education institutions, and agricultural industry groups to enhance coordination and cost-effectiveness.

Focuses on multiple areas, including digital agriculture, artificial intelligence, agricultural biosecurity, cybersecurity, precision agriculture technologies, biotechnology, and crop protection.

Prohibits using funds for new construction or facility alterations.

Requires an annual report on projects, funding, institutional participation, cost-sharing, and intellectual property actions to relevant Congressional committees.

Expands the number of centers from 3 to 8 and extends the program's authorization to 2029.

Official name: Kids Online Safety Act, Title II (Filter Bubble Transparency)
Casual name: Kids Online Safety Act, Title II (Filter Bubble Transparency)
Short summary: Requires platforms using opaque algorithms to notify users about their use and data application and mandates the option to switch to input-transparent algorithms.
Long summary:
Requires online platforms using opaque algorithms to provide users with clear notice about the use of such algorithms and how user-specific data is utilized. 

Mandates platforms to allow users to easily switch between opaque algorithms and input-transparent algorithms. Prohibits differential pricing or service denial based on a user's choice to use an input-transparent algorithm.

Empowers the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to enforce these requirements as violations of unfair or deceptive practices under the Federal Trade Commission Act. 

Ensures that trade secrets, confidential business information, or privileged data are not required to be disclosed. Preserves the ability of platforms to allow users to personalize content restrictions and interactions.
Tags: Risk factors: Interpretability and explainability;Strategies: Disclosure;Strategies: Disclosure: In deployment;Strategies: Disclosure: About inputs

Official name: Border Act of 2024, General Provisions Title
Casual name: Border Act of 2024, General Provisions Title
Short summary: Allocates $170 million for autonomous surveillance towers using AI for border security. Requires open competition for AI-enhanced non-intrusive inspection technology. Defines autonomous systems as those using AI to identify items of interest. Requires quarterly updates on AI technology impacts.
Long summary:
Allocates $170,000,000 for the procurement and deployment of autonomous surveillance tower systems using AI for border security.

Prohibits the use of funds for non-autonomous border security technology.

Defines "autonomous" systems as those utilizing sensors, onboard computing, and AI for identification.

Mandates quarterly reports on the performance and impact of border security technologies, including AI-driven non-intrusive inspection technology.

Requires acquisition of innovative technologies through open competition, integrating AI and machine learning to improve performance.

Obliges U.S. Customs and Border Protection to submit detailed expenditure plans for autonomous systems, including deployment timelines and sustainment costs.

Instructs the Secretary of Homeland Security to conduct cost-benefit analyses before requesting Department of Defense assistance for border operations, including AI technology impacts.

Official name: Government Surveillance Reform Act of 2023, Title VII (Protection of Car Data from Warrantless Searches)
Casual name: Government Surveillance Reform Act of 2023, Title VII (Protection of Car Data from Warrantless Searches)
Short summary: Prohibits law enforcement from accessing vehicle data without a warrant, except with operator consent or in emergencies.
Long summary:
Prohibits law enforcement from accessing vehicle data without a warrant, except with operator consent or in emergencies. 

Defines "covered vehicle data" to include all onboard and telematics data in noncommercial vehicles, excluding certain data types like automotive software and geolocation data for traffic enforcement.
Prohibits the use of any vehicle data obtained in violation of these provisions as evidence in any legal proceedings. Amends the Driver Privacy Act of 2015 to align with these new restrictions on accessing vehicle data.

Official name: Community and Hydropower Improvement Act, Sec. 4 (Approach to Environmental Review)
Casual name: Community and Hydropower Improvement Act, Sec. 4 (Approach to Environmental Review)
Short summary: Amends the Energy Policy Act to require the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission to collaborate with agencies and tribes for environmental assessments. Considers project effects and requires use of open-source models. Evaluates impacts on grid reliability and energy prices.
Long summary:
Amends Section 2403 of the Energy Policy Act of 1992 to enhance environmental review procedures.

Requires the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to cooperate with federal, state, local agencies, and Indian Tribes in preparing environmental assessments or impact statements.

Mandates consideration of ongoing and reasonably foreseeable environmental effects of projects, excluding nonrecurring past effects.

Obliges FERC and cooperating entities to evaluate impacts on fish species and consider appropriate mitigation strategies.

Encourages the use of open-sourced technical models for environmental impact assessments, ensuring their validity and accessibility while protecting intellectual property and proprietary data.

Directs FERC to account for hydrologic alterations over the project license term and assess their impacts on public uses and regional benefits.

Necessitates consideration of innovative solutions and emerging technologies to meet regulatory responsibilities cost-effectively.

Requires periodic technical conferences to evaluate new methodologies and technologies for environmental impact quantification.

Stipulates precise citation of evidence in environmental review records to support findings on material facts.

Official name: A Stronger Workforce for America Act, Chapter 3 (Performance Accountability)
Casual name: A Stronger Workforce for America Act, Chapter 3 (Performance Accountability)
Short summary: Amends the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act to allow states to use machine learning and advanced analytics in the improvement of state program operations and outcomes.
Long summary: Amends the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act to allow states to use machine learning and advanced analytics in the improvement of state program operations and outcomes.
Tags: Applications: Government: other applications/unspecified;Strategies: Government support

Official name: Lummis-Gillibrand Payment Stablecoin Act (Sec. 1-3)
Casual name: Lummis-Gillibrand Payment Stablecoin Act (Sec. 1-3)
Short summary: Prohibits issuing algorithmic payment stablecoins. Requires registration or authorization for issuing payment stablecoins in the U.S. Limits issuance by non-depository trust companies to $10 billion. Establishes safe harbors for limited issuance and foreign regulatory equivalence. Adjusts thresholds for inflation.
Long summary:
Prohibits any person from issuing, creating, or originating an algorithmic payment stablecoin in the United States.

Mandates that payment stablecoins can only be issued by a registered non-depository trust company with a limit on nominal value or an authorized depository institution as a national payment stablecoin issuer.

Prohibits the offer or sale of payment stablecoins in the United States except by a U.S. person living in the U.S.

Requires the Board to adjust the issuance threshold for inflation every four years.

Directs the Board to establish safe harbor regulations, allowing limited issuance of payment stablecoins under specific conditions and fostering innovation and competition.

Allows safe harbor status for issuers regulated by equivalent foreign authorities.

Intends the section to have extraterritorial effect, extending its reach beyond U.S. borders.

Official name: To amend the Communications Act of 1934 to establish technical and procedural standards for artificial or prerecorded voice systems created through generative artificial intelligence, and for other purposes.
Casual name: To amend the Communications Act of 1934 to establish technical and procedural standards for artificial or prerecorded voice systems created through generative artificial intelligence, and for other purposes.
Short summary: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to include technical and procedural standards for artificial or prerecorded voice systems, specifically addressing generative AI technologies like voice cloning.
Long summary:
Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to establish standards for artificial or prerecorded voice systems created through generative AI.

Includes technologies such as voice cloning and other subsequent technologies as deemed appropriate by the Commission.

Inserts these standards into Section 227(d)(3) to ensure compliance with the updated regulatory framework.

Official name: Securing Elections From AI Deception Act
Casual name: Securing Elections From AI Deception Act
Short summary: Prohibits the use of AI to interfere with elections and deprive individuals of the right to vote. Mandates disclaimers on election-related AI-generated content.
Long summary:
Prohibits developers and deployers from using AI to intentionally deceive or defraud individuals of their voting rights in federal, state, or local elections.

Requires election-related AI-generated content to include disclaimers, informing the public that the content was created using AI.

Mandates developers to provide mechanisms ensuring such disclaimers are permanent or difficult to remove.

Directs the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to issue regulations on disclaimer requirements and enforce compliance.

Obliges developers and deployers to ensure their algorithms meet safety and efficiency standards as outlined.

Official name: Promoting Responsible Evaluation and Procurement to Advance Readiness for Enterprise-wide Deployment for Artificial Intelligence Act
Casual name: PREPARED for AI Act
Short summary: Establishes a Chief Artificial Intelligence Officers Council. Mandates risk classification for AI use. Prohibits specific AI activities, including emotion detection. Directs procurement updates. Requires AI risk evaluation and monitoring. Stipulates documentation and transparency. Establishes annual reporting. Prohibits high-risk AI uses.
Long summary:
Requires the establishment of a Chief Artificial Intelligence Officers Council to coordinate AI use in federal agencies.

Mandates agencies to develop risk classification systems for AI use cases, categorizing them as unacceptable, high, medium, or low risk.

Prohibits AI use for certain activities, such as mapping facial features for emotion detection or categorizing individuals based on biometric data for sensitive traits.

Directs updates to the Federal Acquisition Regulation to ensure AI procurement includes risk and impact assessments.

Requires agencies to implement processes for evaluating AI risks and to conduct ongoing monitoring, especially for high-risk use cases.

Stipulates comprehensive documentation and transparency requirements for AI models and data.

Establishes annual reporting protocols for obstacles in AI testing and evaluation, promoting interagency collaboration to address challenges.

Prohibits the use of AI for certain high-risk applications and mandates public disclosure of AI inventories and risk classifications.

Official name: Digital Social Platform Transparency Act
Casual name: Digital Social Platform Transparency Act
Short summary: Requires digital social companies to post and translate terms of service, including content moderation details, within 180 days. Mandates semiannual reports to the Attorney General on terms of service updates and content moderation practices. Imposes penalties for non-compliance.
Long summary:
Mandates digital social companies to post terms of service for each platform within 180 days, detailing contact information, content flagging processes, and potential actions like banning or demonetization.

Requires translation of terms into all supported languages.

Obligates companies to submit semiannual terms of service reports to the Attorney General, detailing content moderation practices and AI involvement, including flagged content statistics and appeal outcomes.

Establishes penalties up to $15,000 per day for non-compliance, managed by the Attorney General, who can take legal action.

Specifies the creation of a public repository of reports for transparency.

Excludes platforms limited to direct messaging or transactions from the Act's scope and protects end-to-end encryption.

Defines key terms and grants the Attorney General interpretive authority.

Ensures Act's obligations and penalties are additional to existing laws.

Official name: To provide for citizen engagement on the development and adoption of Federal civilian agency use of artificial intelligence, and for other purposes.
Casual name: Citizen Engagement on Development of Federal Civilian Agency Use of AI Act
Short summary: Requires the Secretary of Homeland Security to conduct listening sessions with various organizations on AI use by federal agencies. Encourages similar sessions by these entities. Mandates annual reports to Congress on public opinions about federal AI adoption.
Long summary:
Mandates the Secretary of Homeland Security to conduct virtual and in-person listening sessions across the United States for discussing Federal civilian agency use of artificial intelligence.

Requires inclusion of diverse groups in these discussions, such as civil rights organizations, immigrant communities, and academia.

Encourages these groups to hold their own listening sessions and report back on AI development and adoption.

Obligates the Secretary of Homeland Security to submit a report to Congress within 240 days of enactment, and annually thereafter, detailing public attitudes and beliefs on Federal civilian agency use of AI.

Official name: Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act
Casual name: Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act
Short summary: Establishes regulatory sandboxes for financial entities to test AI projects with reduced regulatory burden. Requires applications detailing compliance strategies, potential impacts, and public interest benefits. Mandates annual reports on outcomes and allows agencies to create additional AI project regulations post-approval.
Long summary:
Establishes regulatory sandboxes allowing financial entities to experiment with AI test projects under reduced regulatory burdens.

Requires financial entities to apply for sandbox participation by detailing their AI project, compliance strategies, and potential public benefits.

Mandates applications include risk assessments for systemic financial stability and national security.

Obligates financial regulatory agencies to review applications within 90 days, with provisions for approval, denial, or extension.

Allows joint applications by multiple entities and necessitates notification to all relevant regulatory bodies.

Stipulates data security measures for information provided by AI project sponsors.

Directs each financial regulatory agency to promulgate regulations, including modification procedures and confidentiality protocols, within 180 days of the Act’s enactment.

Mandates annual reporting to Congress on AI test project outcomes.

Permits regulatory agencies to establish additional AI projects as qualifying test projects after public consultation.

Official name: Social Media and AI Resiliency Toolkits in Schools Act
Casual name: SMART in Schools Act
Short summary: Mandates development and updates of AI and social media impact toolkits on youth. Requires stakeholder consultation. Specifies evidence-based content on digital resilience. Directs tailored guidance inclusion. Instructs dissemination through institutions. Authorizes $2,000,000 for implementation.
Long summary:
Mandates the Secretaries of Education and Health & Human Services to develop educational toolkits about artificial intelligence (AI) and social media's impact on youth.

Requires consultation with various relevant stakeholders, including students, parents & guardians, educators, administrators, and healthcare professionals, to develop the toolkits.

Specifies guidelines for each different type of toolkit, to be appropriately targeted to their multiple intended audiences: students, parents & guardians, educators, administrators, and healthcare professionals.

Directs the Secretaries to develop toolkit dissemination strategies, so that the resources reach their intended audiences.

Authorizes $2,000,000 for facilitating the dissemination of the toolkits.

Official name: Supporting Innovation in Agriculture Act of 2024
Casual name: Supporting Innovation in Agriculture Act of 2024
Short summary: Establishes a 30% tax credit for investments in innovative agricultural technology, including AI systems, precision agriculture, and controlled environment agriculture, effective for property construction starting after January 1, 2023. Defines eligible technology types and project criteria.
Long summary:
Establishes a 30% tax credit for investments in innovative agricultural technology projects.

Defines qualified property to include tangible personal property, software, and computer systems used in such projects.

Specifies that projects must be placed in service before December 31, 2035, and focus on precision or controlled environment agriculture.

Includes technologies such as AI, machine learning, robotics, and automated systems within the scope of eligible projects.

Allows taxpayers to elect a payment in lieu of the credit for qualifying projects.

Prohibits double benefits for projects also funded by certain federal grants.

Applies the amendments to property construction beginning after January 1, 2023.

Official name: Supercritical Geothermal Research and Development Act
Casual name: Supercritical Geothermal Research and Development Act
Short summary: Amends the Energy Independence and Security Act to incorporate machine learning in geothermal research. Establishes a supercritical geothermal research program focusing on supercritical conditions. Requires periodic updates to geothermal data repositories. Authorizes funding for research and development through 2030.
Long summary:
Amends the Energy Independence and Security Act of 2007 to include machine learning algorithms in geothermal research and development.

Establishes a supercritical geothermal research and development program within the Geothermal Technologies Office, focusing on well completion, permeability, materials, sensors, and water-rock geochemistry.

Requires the creation of a repository for geothermal data, with periodic updates for data standardization and accessibility.

Mandates a memorandum of understanding for data sharing regarding geothermal development among federal departments.

Directs the Secretary of the Interior to drill deep exploration boreholes for geothermal mapping.

Establishes a next-generation geothermal center of excellence to coordinate research, development, and workforce initiatives.

Authorizes $5 million annually from 2026 to 2030 for these activities.

Updates geothermal resource assessments quadrennially to include supercritical geothermal regions.

Requires progress reports on geothermal resource potential and commercialization barriers.

Official name: Nucleic Acid Standards for Biosecurity Act
Casual name: Nucleic Acid Standards for Biosecurity Act
Short summary: Amends the law to develop best practices and technical standards for managing AI-related risks in engineering biology. Establishes a consortium to create guidelines for nucleic acid synthesis screening. Allocates $5 million annually for 2025-2029 for these activities.
Long summary:
Amends the Research and Development, Competition, and Innovation Act to include best practices and technical standards for managing risks in engineering biology and biomanufacturing, specifically addressing artificial intelligence.

Requires the Director, in consultation with Federal agencies, to conduct measurement research supporting biosecurity measures related to nucleic acid synthesis.

Mandates testing to improve screening accuracy, efficacy, and reliability, along with best practices for operational security and database management.

Directs the development of technical guidance for effective and secure screening and conformity-assessment standards.

Establishes a consortium of stakeholders to develop and update best practices and roadmaps for synthetic nucleic acid procurement screening.

Requires the consortium to report its findings to Congress within 18 months of its first meeting.

Authorizes $5,000,000 annually for fiscal years 2025 through 2029 for scientific and technical research activities under this initiative.

Official name: MedShield Act of 2024
Casual name: MedShield Act of 2024
Short summary: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to implement the MedShield program using AI for pandemic preparedness. Leverages AI for pathogen surveillance, vaccine and therapeutic development. Allocates funding from 2025 to 2029 for program execution.
Long summary:
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to implement the MedShield program, utilizing artificial intelligence for pandemic preparedness and response.

Mandates the use of AI for developing a global pathogen surveillance system, accelerating vaccine development, and optimizing pathogen tracking and therapeutic interventions.

Obligates the Secretary to integrate recommendations from the National Security Commission on Artificial Intelligence and coordinate with federal agencies and international partners.

Directs the development of a plan to enhance public health and medical services as part of the National Response Framework.

Sets funding authorization at increasing annual amounts from $300 million in fiscal year 2025 to $500 million in fiscal year 2029.

Requires submission of a detailed report on the program's plan to relevant Congressional committees within 180 days of the Act's enactment.

Official name: Civilian Agency AI Watermark Act
Casual name: Civilian Agency AI Watermark Act
Short summary: Requires the Secretary of Homeland Security to consult with relevant directors to develop an AI watermark system for civilian agency applications. Authorizes necessary appropriations annually for this purpose.
Long summary:
Mandates the Secretary of Homeland Security to consult with the Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology and the Director of the United States Patent and Trademark Office within eight months to develop an AI watermark and trademark system for identifying Federal agency-approved AI applications for civilian agency use.

Authorizes the appropriation of necessary funds each fiscal year to the Secretary of Homeland Security to implement this AI watermark system.

Official name: AI Leadership To Enable Accountable Deployment Act
Casual name: AI LEAD Act
Short summary: Establishes the Chief AI Officers Council to promote and coordinate AI innovation and risk management. Requires each agency to appoint a CAIO responsible for developing and implementing AI strategies.
Long summary:
Establishes the Chief Artificial Intelligence Officers Council to promote AI innovation, oversee compliance, and advise on best practices for AI technologies.

Requires each agency to designate a Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer responsible for promoting AI use, managing risks, and ensuring transparency and privacy.

Mandates the creation of Artificial Intelligence Coordination Boards within agencies to address AI issues and publish guiding principles and goals.

Directs agencies to develop an AI strategy focused on responsible and trustworthy adoption, public trust, risk mitigation, and interagency coordination.

Requires the Comptroller General to report on the effectiveness of AI Coordination Boards, evaluate Chief AI Officers, and assess AI costs, benefits, and risks, including job impacts.

Directs the Office of Management and Budget Director to issue updated AI governance guidance within five years.

Official name: NSF and USDA Interagency Research Act
Casual name: NSF and USDA Interagency Research Act
Short summary: Require collaborative AI research by the USDA and NSF, emphasizing emerging technologies like AI, machine learning, and robotics for agriculture. Establish memoranda of understanding, promote data sharing, and support infrastructure and workforce development. Report on achievements and opportunities biennially.
Long summary:
Mandates the Secretary of Agriculture and the Director of the National Science Foundation to conduct collaborative research and development activities, including those involving artificial intelligence and digital agriculture, to advance their mission priorities.

Requires the establishment of memoranda of understanding to coordinate these activities through competitive, merit-based processes.

Permits collaborative research in key areas, such as AI, machine learning, automation, and digital agriculture.

Encourages data sharing and collaboration among federal agencies, educational institutions, and nonprofit entities.

Supports research infrastructure and the development of translational technologies for commercial use.

Facilitates public-private partnerships and awards grants to educational institutions to establish a Center for Agricultural Research, Education, and Workforce Development.

Authorizes reimbursable agreements to enhance research effectiveness and mandates a report to Congress on interagency coordination and future collaboration opportunities.

Ensures research security consistent with the CHIPS Act of 2022.

Official name: S.4870, A bill to require reports on artificial intelligence regulation in the financial services industry.
Casual name: S.4870, A bill to require reports on artificial intelligence regulation in the financial services industry.
Short summary: Require federal financial agencies to report to Congress on AI knowledge gaps, usage, governance standards, regulatory needs, interagency issues, and resource requirements within 90 days. Exclude confidential supervisory and nonpublic information from reports.
Long summary:
Require the Federal Reserve, FDIC, OCC, NCUA, and CFPB to report on AI knowledge gaps in financial services within 90 days.

Mandate analysis on AI-assisted tasks in regulated institutions.

Assess current AI governance and oversight standards.

Identify additional regulatory authorities needed for effective mission execution.

Clarify overlapping AI regulatory issues between agencies.

Evaluate current and planned agency use of AI over the next three years, including fiscal and staffing impacts.

Determine necessary resources to adapt to AI changes and oversee its use.

Ensure reports exclude confidential or nonpublic information.

Official name: H.R. 9626, To direct the Department of Defense to develop a plan for the establishment of a secure computing and data storage environment for the testing of artificial intelligence trained on biological data, and for other purposes.
Casual name: H.R. 9626, DOD Secure Computing and Data Storage Plan Development
Short summary: Establishes a secure AIxBio sandbox for testing AI trained on biological data. Designates the Under Secretary of Defense for Research and Engineering to manage it. Involves selecting defense-applicable projects and facilitating partnerships with DoD and academic labs. Requires reporting to Congress.
Long summary:
Mandates the Under Secretary of Defense for Research and Engineering to develop a plan for a secure computing and data storage environment, termed the "AIxBio sandbox," to facilitate the testing of AI models trained on biological data.

Requires the AIxBio sandbox to utilize existing Department of Defense infrastructure when feasible and designates the Under Secretary of Defense for managing and overseeing its activities.

Directs the Under Secretary to select projects for the sandbox that demonstrate potential defense applications at the intersection of AI and biotechnology, such as medical countermeasures and biomanufacturing.

Establishes mechanisms for collaboration between Department of Defense laboratories and academic institutions to support AIxBio projects, with streamlined processes for efficient partnerships.

Obligates the Under Secretary to report and brief Congress on the plan within one year of the Act's enactment.

Official name: National Windstorm Impact Reduction Program Reauthorization Act of 2024
Casual name: National Windstorm Impact Reduction Program Reauthorization Act of 2024
Short summary: Integrates machine learning and advanced technologies into windstorm research, supporting interdisciplinary collaborations. Enhances understanding of climate change impacts on windstorms. Allocates funding for research, infrastructure, and resilience measures to mitigate windstorm effects across diverse communities.
Long summary:
Amends the National Windstorm Impact Reduction Act of 2004 to include provisions for understanding climate variability and its impact on windstorms, aiming to inform future building codes.

Supports the integration of emerging technologies, such as machine learning, in research and engineering activities related to windstorm mitigation.

Encourages interdisciplinary research, including collaborations between social scientists and engineers, to enhance atmospheric measurements relevant to building engineering.

Promotes the use of advanced construction methods and materials to strengthen properties against windstorms and related impacts.

Mandates an analysis of the need for a tornado research facility to study tornado impacts on buildings.

Requires research into public response to windstorm risks and factors influencing risk communication and reduction measures.

Allocates specific funding amounts for the program from fiscal years 2025 to 2029.

Sets deadlines for reports and analyses, including future research infrastructure needs, to be completed by specified dates.

Official name: State Industrial Competitiveness Act of 2024
Casual name: State Industrial Competitiveness Act of 2024
Short summary: Amends the Energy Policy and Conservation Act to establish a Flex-Tech Energy Program. Provides financial assistance for manufacturers to enhance competitiveness, including AI adoption. Allocates funding, emphasizes technical assistance, and mandates a minimum 5% funding for Indian Tribes.
Long summary:
Amends the Energy Policy and Conservation Act to establish the Flex-Tech Energy Program aimed at enhancing manufacturing competitiveness.

Requires the Secretary to provide financial assistance to State energy agencies or Indian Tribes for developing and implementing flex-tech programs, including measures related to advanced manufacturing technologies and artificial intelligence.

Mandates that energy studies of manufacturing facilities evaluate systems, emissions, and potential efficiency measures, including AI applications.

Allocates at least 5% of funds specifically for Indian Tribes or manufacturers in Indian Country.

Limits financial assistance usage, capping administrative expenses at 10% and individual facility funding at $100,000 or 5% of total assistance.

Encourages the use of both public and private financing in program implementation.

Authorizes $100 million annually from 2025 to 2029 for the program.

Official name: Synthetic Biology Advancement Act of 2024
Casual name: Synthetic Biology Advancement Act of 2024
Short summary: Establishes a National Synthetic Biology Center to award grants for projects integrating AI and synthetic biology in agriculture. Prioritizes research on gene editing, digital agriculture, and AI applications. Requires reporting on funded research and innovation progress to Congress biennially.
Long summary:
Establishes the National Synthetic Biology Center to award grants for advancing synthetic biology in agriculture.

Requires the Center to prioritize research in areas like cellular biology, gene editing, and digital agriculture.

Mandates the use of artificial intelligence to accelerate modeling and measurement for synthetic biological solutions.

Provides grants to eligible institutions, fostering partnerships with entities such as nonprofits and national laboratories.

Sets criteria for grant applications, emphasizing the promotion of synthetic biology technologies and sustainability in agriculture.

Directs the Center to coordinate with technology transfer offices to commercialize innovations.

Requires the Center to maintain a website for disseminating findings and facilitating collaboration.

Obligates the Center to submit biennial reports to Congress on research findings and grant progress.

Authorizes appropriations for the Center's operation and grant awards from 2025 to 2029.

Official name: PATHS Act
Casual name: PATHS Act
Short summary: Extends the authority for AI-related transactions under the Homeland Security Act to 2027. Requires notification and briefing to Congress within 72 hours of using AI transaction authority. Reduces covered contract award amounts from $4,000,000 to $1,000,000.
Long summary:
Extends the research and development acquisition pilot program under the Homeland Security Act of 2002 to September 30, 2027.

Mandates the Secretary of Homeland Security to notify and brief Congress within 72 hours regarding the use or extension of transaction authority involving artificial intelligence technology.

Reduces the threshold for covered contract awards from $4,000,000 to $1,000,000 under the James M. Inhofe National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2023.

Official name: AI Innovation and Development for Efficiency Act of 2024
Casual name: AI Innovation and Development for Efficiency Act of 2024
Short summary: Requires the National Science Foundation to support research on AI-enabled efficient technologies, focusing on smart grids, transportation, and emissions reduction. Emphasizes impact assessments and prioritizes projects at EPSCoR institutions. Mandates reporting findings and recommendations to Congress.
Long summary:
Mandates the National Science Foundation Director to support research on AI-enabled efficient technologies through merit-reviewed and competitively awarded projects.

Requires consideration of projects focusing on smart technologies for infrastructure efficiency, pollution minimization, and upgrading existing constructions.

Obliges researchers to conduct impact assessments of energy consumption for AI-enabled technologies.

Prioritizes research conducted at EPSCoR institutions.

Directs the Director to report findings and recommendations on research opportunities and coordination with federal agencies and the private sector within 18 months of enactment.

Requires a report on research results within four years to relevant Senate and House committees.

Official name: A bill to establish protections for individual rights with respect to computational algorithms, and for other purposes.
Casual name: Artificial Intelligence Civil Rights Act of 2024
Short summary: Prohibits discriminatory use of AI algorithms and mandates pre- and post-deployment evaluations. Requires transparency in algorithm use. Enforces compliance through Federal Trade Commission oversight, state actions, and private lawsuits. Establishes resources for algorithm auditing and consumer awareness initiatives.
Long summary:
Prohibits developers and deployers of AI algorithms from causing or contributing to discrimination based on protected characteristics.

Mandates developers and deployers to conduct pre-deployment evaluations and annual impact assessments of AI algorithms to identify potential harms or disparate impacts.

Requires independent audits of AI algorithms and obligates developers to provide relevant information to deployers for compliance.

Imposes transparency obligations, including public disclosures of AI practices and consumer rights, in multiple languages and accessible formats.

Grants the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) enforcement authority, including rulemaking, with violations treated as unfair or deceptive acts.

Provides a private right of action for individuals, enabling lawsuits for violations and barring pre-dispute arbitration agreements.

Establishes federal resources for algorithm auditing, including a new occupational series and additional personnel for the FTC to handle AI-related issues.

Official name: Bureau of Industry and Security Information Technology Modernization Act
Casual name: BIS IT Modernization Act
Short summary: Requires the Bureau of Industry and Security to integrate AI, machine learning, and advanced tools into IT systems. Mandates data fusion and analytics for export controls. Enhances data sharing and security. Allocates $25 million annually through 2028 for modernization.
Long summary:
Mandates the Bureau of Industry and Security to adopt advanced data fusion, analytics, and AI capabilities to enhance export control processes and identify evasive trade patterns.

Requires the integration of AI and machine learning into Bureau IT systems to improve decision-making and enforce export controls effectively.

Obligates the Bureau to automate and streamline data sharing with industry, federal agencies, and international partners.

Demands the modernization of IT systems to support seamless data management, enhance security, and facilitate the use of cutting-edge technology.

Stipulates objectives for technology adoption, including productivity enhancement, cost reduction, and improved user experience for U.S. exporters.

Instructs the Under Secretary to reassess staffing needs and consult Congress on personnel adjustments during the modernization.

Authorizes $25 million annually from 2025 to 2028 for these initiatives.

Official name: Transformational Artificial intelligence to Modernize the Economy against Extreme Weather Act
Casual name: TAME Extreme Weather Act
Short summary: Develops advanced AI applications for weather forecasting, enhances AI weather model integration, and improves emissions monitoring. Supports AI use to predict extreme weather impacts and fires. Encourages public-private partnerships and workforce development, ensuring data access while safeguarding national security and intellectual property.
Long summary:
Mandates the development of comprehensive weather forecasting training datasets using AI to improve Earth system modeling and weather forecasts.

Requires collaboration with federal agencies and experts to enhance AI weather models and explore AI's role in public preparedness and resilience.

Directs the use of AI for fire environment modeling to predict, detect, and forecast wildfires and associated hazards.

Establishes an emissions monitoring program using AI to analyze global atmospheric data and improve greenhouse gas emission models.

Encourages partnerships with private and academic entities to advance AI in weather forecasting and environmental predictions.

Calls for recruitment and development of a diverse workforce skilled in AI applications for weather forecasting.

Allows public access to data and code developed under this Act, with safeguards for national security and intellectual property.

Supports ongoing research and data acquisition for AI and numerical weather models, ensuring continued advancement in weather prediction technologies.

Official name: S.4862, A bill to ensure that new advances in artificial intelligence are ethically adopted to improve the health of all individuals, and for other purposes.
Casual name: S.4862, A bill to ensure that new advances in artificial intelligence are ethically adopted to improve the health of all individuals, and for other purposes.
Short summary: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require NIH to develop AI resources and datasets for health research, and to provide education for ethical AI use. Requires hospitals to request patients' consent for EHR data sharing. Allocates $660 million for AI initiatives.
Long summary:
Amends the Public Health Service Act to mandate the NIH Director develop AI resources and datasets for health research, provide AI expertise, and maintain data access resources.

Authorizes $400 million for NIH AI initiatives in fiscal year 2025.

Directs the National Coordinator for Health IT to utilize electronic health records for research, establish data standards, and align with national interoperability frameworks.

Appropriates $10 million for data collection and $50 million for data standards.

Requires hospitals to request patient consent to use health records for research, contingent upon patient approval.

Expresses the Senate's view that NIH representation is crucial in steering committees for national AI research resources.

Mandates the National Library of Medicine to establish data-sharing facilities and create a core data science program.

Appropriates $100 million each for these library initiatives in fiscal year 2025.

Official name: Modernizing Data Practices to Improve Government Act
Casual name: Modernizing Data Practices to Improve Government Act
Short summary: Extends the Chief Data Officer Council's authority to improve federal data governance. Enables agencies to securely adopt AI by appointing Chief AI Officers and mandating reports on data governance best practices, AI use cases, and synthetic data security.
Long summary:
Amends section 3520A of title 44, United States Code, to extend the Chief Data Officer Council's authority to improve Federal data governance.

Requires the Council to identify opportunities to enhance data governance for reliable and secure adoption of emerging technologies and AI.

Mandates appointment of a representative from Chief Artificial Intelligence Officers to the Council.

Obligates the Council to submit biennial reports detailing recommendations for developing datasets and data governance policies to support AI adoption.

Instructs the Council to assess data governance challenges, clarify roles among senior agency leaders, and recommend best practices for AI data use.

Requires recommendations for data ownership policies to ensure government rights and pre-procurement AI reviews.

Directs the Director to issue guidance based on Council reports.

Mandates an annual data management report to Congress clarifying Chief Data Officers' roles in AI governance.

Sunsets the amendments seven years after enactment.

Official name: Consumer Literacy and Empowerment to Advance Responsible Navigation of Artificial Intelligence Act
Casual name: Consumers LEARN AI Act
Short summary: Directs the Secretary of Commerce to develop a national strategy and conduct a public campaign in collaboration with federal, state, and industry partners to enhance AI consumer literacy and promote understanding of AI capabilities and limitations for informed decision making.
Long summary:
Directs the Secretary of Commerce to develop a national strategy and and conduct a public service campaign to enhance artificial intelligence consumer literacy.

Requires the strategy to promote understanding of AI capabilities and limitations, and support informed decisions on AI use.

Instructs the Secretary to coordinate with various federal agencies, local governments, and private organizations to ensure comprehensive strategy development.

Requires the creation of specialized use cases for AI literacy in domains like healthcare, finance, and communication.
 
Mandates the dissemination of educational materials through multiple communication channels, including a dedicated website, and requires periodic updates to these materials.

Obliges the Government Accountability Office to report on existing federal AI literacy programs and the Secretary to annually report on the strategy's progress and effectiveness.

Authorizes necessary appropriations for fiscal years 2025 through 2034 to implement the Act's activities.

Official name: Ending FCC Meddling in Our Elections Act
Casual name: Ending FCC Meddling in Our Elections Act
Short summary: Prohibits the Federal Communications Commission from creating or enforcing rules about disclosing AI-generated content in political advertisements, as outlined in a May 22, 2024, draft proposal by the Commission's Chairwoman.
Long summary:
Prohibits the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) from promulgating or enforcing any rules related to the disclosure of artificial intelligence-generated content in political advertisements.

Restricts the FCC specifically from acting on the draft notice of proposed rulemaking circulated by the Chairwoman on May 22, 2024, concerning AI-generated content disclosure in political ads across television, radio, or other services.

Bars the FCC from enacting any rules substantially similar to the aforementioned proposal.

Official name: AI Advancement and Reliability Act of 2024
Casual name: AI Advancement and Reliability Act of 2024
Short summary: Establishes the Center for AI Advancement and Reliability to lead efforts in AI research and development, and the evaluation of AI systems' robustness, resilience, and safety.
Long summary:
Establishes the Center for AI Advancement and Reliability to ensure U.S. leadership in AI research, development, and evaluation.

Outlines activities of the Center including conducting measurement research to develop best practices for AI reliability and safety, evaluating AI capabilities and developing methods for risk management.

Mandates the Under Secretary of Commerce for Standards and Technology to coordinate with federal agencies in establishing the Center.

Creates a consortium of stakeholders to support the Center and the National Institute of Standards and Technology in AI-related activities.

Authorizes $10 million for fiscal year 2025 for the establishment of the Center.

Official name: AI Transparency in Elections Act of 2024
Casual name: AI Transparency in Elections Act of 2024
Short summary: Mandates disclaimers on political ads containing AI-generated content, establishes penalties for non-compliance, and requires the Federal Election Commission to issue regulations and periodic reports on enforcement and compliance.
Long summary:
Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act to require disclaimers on political advertisements containing content generated by AI.

Outlines requirements for clear disclosure in ads using AI-generated images, audio, or video, specifying format requirements for visibility and audibility.

Imposes penalties for non-compliance, with fines up to $50,000 per violation, and establishes enforcement mechanisms.

Obliges the Federal Election Commission to issue regulations within 90 days, outlining criteria for AI content identification and disclosure requirements.

Requires the FEC to report biannually on compliance and enforcement, providing recommendations for improvements.
